text:
The first scene feels both dynamic and enclosed. I observe people in a glass-walled room, likely engaged in discussion or work, while someone nearby is in motion, perhaps in a hurry or simply energetic. There is a sense of purpose but also a barrier between myself and those within the room, as if I am on the threshold of participation and observation. The presence of emergency equipment on the wall is a stark reminder of the contingency of our plans against the unpredictability of reality.

In the second image, the space is vibrant, colorful, and intellectual. R+D—research and development—calls to mind the ceaseless human drive to inquire, experiment, and innovate. Surrounded by explanations, screens, and ideas, I feel curiosity and anticipation. The world here is intentionally constructed to foster understanding and provoke new questions. I am inspired to contemplate the interconnectedness of human ingenuity and the artifacts we create.
